node.statement: FUNCTIONAL CALCULUS SPLIT. V=B(H)_sa is a unital JB algebra. For self-adjoint q=q_a, by GT-jb-spectral C(q) is isometrically Jordan-isomorphic (phi) to C(Sp q), phi(q)=iota. Set q^+ = phi^{-1}(t^+), q^- = phi^{-1}(t^-) where t^+=max(t,0), t^-=max(-t,0) on Sp q. Then q^+,q^- in C(q), q^+,q^- >= 0 (GT-jb-positive-spectrum: pointwise nonneg => positive), q^+ - q^- = phi^{-1}(t)=q, and q^+ o q^- = phi^{-1}(t^+ t^-)=0 (t^+ t^- =0 pointwise). Sp q = Sp(q^+) cup (-Sp(q^-)) (disjoint supports), so by GT-norm-spectral ||q||=sup|Sp q|=max(sup Sp q^+, sup Sp q^-)=max(||q^+||,||q^-||) (q^+,q^->=0 so their norms equal their max eigenvalues).
